functional_requirements: The target solution shall preserve 212 detected route-level workflows or approved replacements.
The target solution shall externalize controller logic into maintainable application services.
The target solution shall preserve or replace 153 extracted SQL touchpoints through secure, parameterized data access.
The target solution shall reimplement custom MySQL functions recentProfile(), alignRate() in the target data or service layer before ORM and query migration is finalized.
The target solution shall preserve required authentication, authorization, and session-based workflow controls.
non_functional_requirements: Target architecture shall provide auditable route-to-service traceability.
Security-sensitive data handling shall use parameterized queries and protected session/auth controls.
Validation behavior shall be explicit and testable in the target implementation.
compliance_requirements: Access control behavior must be preserved or explicitly redesigned with approval.
File upload and export flows must be governed by validation and secure handling controls.
business_rules: Only authenticated and authorized users may access protected operational workflows.
Input validation must be applied before data updates or file operations are committed.
display_requirements: Web workflows must provide clear navigation, input capture, and error feedback for users.
Validation and authorization failures must be expressed in business-readable language.
validations: 58 files contain explicit validation signals and must be reflected in target-state validation rules.
notifications: Authentication, validation, and file-operation failures must surface clear user feedback.
